questions.question: What does the Partial<User> utility type do?
options: Makes some fields of User required
Makes all fields of User optional
Removes some fields from User
Creates a partial copy of a User object
questions.answer: Makes all fields of User optional
questions.explanation: Partial<T> transforms all properties of T into optional ones, useful for update DTOs where you might want to update only some fields.

questions.question: What does K extends keyof T constrain in a generic function?
options: K must be a primitive type
K must be a valid property name (key) of type T
K must extend the T class
K must be a string or number
questions.answer: K must be a valid property name (key) of type T
questions.explanation: K extends keyof T constrains K to be one of the property names of T, ensuring type-safe property access like getProperty(obj, key) where key must be a real key.

questions.question: What is the difference between Pick<User, 'id' | 'name'> and Omit<User, 'password'>?
options: They are identical
Pick creates a type with ONLY the listed fields; Omit creates a type with all fields EXCEPT the listed ones
Pick removes fields; Omit keeps them
Pick works on arrays; Omit works on objects
questions.answer: Pick creates a type with ONLY the listed fields; Omit creates a type with all fields EXCEPT the listed ones
questions.explanation: Pick<User, 'id' | 'name'> creates { id, name }. Omit<User, 'password'> creates all User fields minus 'password'. Use Pick when you want few fields; Omit when you want most.

questions.question: What does the 'infer' keyword do in conditional types?
options: Makes TypeScript infer the type automatically
Allows you to extract and name a type from within a conditional type check
Infers the return type of a function
Creates a type inference rule
questions.answer: Allows you to extract and name a type from within a conditional type check
questions.explanation: infer E within T extends (infer E)[] ? E : never creates a new type variable E and binds it to the array element type, enabling extraction of nested types.

questions.question: What is a mapped type in TypeScript?
options: A Map collection with typed keys and values
A type created by iterating over keys of another type and transforming them
A function that maps one type to another
An array type with fixed elements
questions.answer: A type created by iterating over keys of another type and transforming them
questions.explanation: Mapped types use [K in keyof T] syntax to transform each property: type Nullable<T> = { [K in keyof T]: T[K] | null }; creates a new type with every property nullable.
